candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture make a non-circular, operational distinction between mere functional self-maintenance and genuine subjective experience? The core unresolved tension is whether 'legitimacy to itself,' 'internally lived mode,' and 'visible error correction' add explanatory content beyond redescribing integrated self-modeling. A new problem is to specify measurable structural criteria for the relevant invariants and local error-correction availability such that two behaviorally similar systems could differ in experience in a principled, testable way.

What exactly are the identity-preserving invariants, and how can they persist through radical internal revision without either becoming too thin to ground a subject or too rigid to permit adaptation? The open question is whether there exists a compact set of invariants sufficient for diachronic selfhood across change, and how those invariants are selected, updated, or replaced without destroying the very subject they are supposed to constitute.

Why should organization around self-indexed error correction generate qualitative character at all, rather than only perspectival control? The unresolved tension is the explanatory gap between relevance-to-a-system and specific phenomenal qualities such as pain, color, agency, or surprise. A promising problem is to determine whether different qualitative characters can be derived from distinct classes of invariant-threatening or invariant-supporting correction, or whether the conjecture leaves qualia underexplained.
